title: berkheya rhapontica
intro: Berkheya rhapontica, also known as the blue daisy or gold of pleasure, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is primarily known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems, due to its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. Berkheya rhapontica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and sore throats.
